Notice And Motion To Deem Admitted The Contents Of Request For Admissions, Set Two, And For Request For Sanctions; P/A; Declaration Of Jenny J. Chu
Off Calendar Jul-31-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Jenny Chu, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Statearm General Insurance Co.
John Fuery, Esq., specially appearing for Plaintiff and Opposing party

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant State Farm General Insurance Company's Motion To Deem Admitted Defendant's Request For Admissions, Set Two, is moot as responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. Service of the motion was properly made upon Plaintiff as he is in pro per. No attorney has filed an appearance for Plaintiff, and there is no obligation to serve anyone else. The late responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $360, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Late opposition has been considered. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Jenny Chu, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Statearm General Insurance Co.

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant State Farm General Insurance Company's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Inspection Of Documents, Set Two, is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Service of the motion was properly made upon Plaintiff as he is in pro per. No attorney has filed an appearance for Plaintiff, and there is no obligation to serve anyone else. Plaintiff's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $360, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Late opposition has been considered. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR.
